entry.title: Auth0
entry.description: Auth0 provides authentication and authorization as a service for web, mobile, and legacy applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes up to 7,000 active users and unlimited logins per month.
caveats: No SLA or phone support
Some advanced features require paid plans

useCases: Add secure login to web or mobile apps
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Auth0 account.
Create a new Application in the Auth0 dashboard.
Configure allowed callback URLs and application type.
Integrate Auth0 SDK into your app using provided credentials.
Test login and user flows in your application.
entry.whenToUse: Use Auth0 when you need a quick, secure authentication solution with minimal setup for web or mobile apps.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Auth0 if you require full on-premise control, advanced customization, or need to support more than 7,000 users for free.
entry.body: Auth0 offers a robust authentication platform with a generous free tier, supporting social logins, MFA, and easy integration for most app types.

entry.title: Authgear
entry.description: Authgear provides user authentication, social login, and multi-factor authentication as a managed service.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes up to 1,000 monthly active users with core authentication features.
caveats: No custom domain on free tier
Limited to community support

useCases: Add authentication to web or mobile apps
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Authgear account.
Create a new application in the Authgear dashboard.
Configure authentication methods (email, social logins, MFA).
Integrate Authgear SDK into your app.
Test user signup and login flows.
entry.whenToUse: Use Authgear when you need managed authentication with social login and MFA for apps with up to 1,000 users.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require custom domains, advanced enterprise features, or expect to exceed 1,000 monthly active users on the free tier.
entry.body: Authgear offers a managed authentication platform with a generous free tier for small projects, supporting social login, MFA, and easy SDK integration.

entry.title: Authress
entry.description: Authress provides user authentication, authorization, and identity management as a service for modern applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes up to 1,000 monthly active users with core authentication and authorization features.
caveats: Advanced features like enterprise SSO and audit logs require paid plans
API rate limits may apply

useCases: Add secure login to web or mobile apps
quickstartSteps: Sign up for an Authress account at https://authress.io/.
Create a new application in the Authress dashboard.
Configure authentication providers (e.g., Google, GitHub) as needed.
Integrate the Authress SDK into your app using provided documentation.
Test user login and access control flows.
entry.whenToUse: Use Authress when you need a managed authentication and authorization solution for apps without building your own identity infrastructure.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require on-premise deployment, advanced enterprise integrations, or need full control over authentication infrastructure.
entry.body: Authress delivers managed authentication and authorization services, enabling secure user login, SSO, and access control for modern applications with minimal setup.

entry.title: Authy
entry.description: Authy provides two-factor authentication (2FA) services for securing user logins and transactions.

freeTierDetails.summary: Authy offers a limited free tier for 2FA via SMS, voice, and push authentication.
limits: Includes SMS, voice, and push notifications
caveats: Overages billed per authentication
Phone verification may incur charges
billingRiskNotes: Charges apply if monthly free limit is exceeded

useCases: Add 2FA to web or mobile apps
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Twilio account.
Create an Authy application in the Twilio Console.
Integrate Authy SDK or API into your app.
Configure authentication methods (SMS, voice, push).
Test 2FA flows with test users.
entry.whenToUse: Use Authy when you need to add multi-factor authentication to your app with minimal setup and broad device support.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Authy if you require a fully free, unlimited 2FA solution or need on-premises deployment.
entry.body: Authy by Twilio enables developers to add secure two-factor authentication to applications using SMS, voice, or push notifications, with a generous free tier for low-volume use.

entry.title: Azure Active Directory B2C
entry.description: Azure Active Directory B2C provides cloud-based identity and access management for consumer-facing applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 50,000 monthly active users with basic authentication features.
caveats: Premium features require paid tier
Custom policies may incur charges
billingRiskNotes: Charges apply if monthly active users exceed 50,000

useCases: Enable social and enterprise logins for web or mobile apps
Implement user self-service sign-up and password reset
quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure portal and create an Azure AD B2C tenant.
Link your B2C tenant to your Azure subscription.
Register your application in the B2C tenant.
Configure user flows (e.g., sign-up, sign-in, password reset).
Integrate the B2C authentication endpoints into your application.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need scalable, managed authentication for consumer-facing apps with support for social and enterprise identity providers.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced custom policies or need on-premises-only identity solutions.
entry.body: Azure Active Directory B2C offers a scalable, managed authentication platform for consumer applications, supporting social, enterprise, and local account sign-ins with a generous free tier.

entry.title: Firebase Auth
entry.description: Firebase Auth provides authentication services for web and mobile apps with support for multiple identity providers.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes generous monthly quotas for authentication operations.
limits: 10k verifications/month for email/password, anonymous, and social providers
caveats: Phone authentication quota is low; overages are billed.
Some advanced features (multi-factor, SAML, OIDC) may have additional costs.
billingRiskNotes: Phone authentication overages can incur charges.
Monitor usage to avoid unexpected costs.

useCases: Add user authentication to web or mobile apps
quickstartSteps: Create a Firebase project in the Firebase Console.
Enable the desired authentication providers (email/password, Google, etc.).
Add Firebase SDK to your app.
Configure authentication methods in your app code.
Test sign-up and sign-in flows.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need quick, secure authentication for web or mobile apps with minimal backend setup.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require on-premise hosting, custom auth flows, or strict data residency requirements.
entry.body: Firebase Auth offers a simple way to add authentication to your apps, supporting multiple providers and scalable user management with a generous free tier.

entry.title: LoginLlama
entry.description: LoginLlama provides free suspicious login detection and webhook alerts for authentication flows.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes suspicious login detection and webhook notifications for up to 10,000 requests per month.
caveats: Advanced features require paid plan
No SLA on free tier

useCases: Integrate fraud alerts into authentication flows
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free LoginLlama account.
Create a new application in the dashboard.
Copy your API key and webhook URL.
Integrate the LoginLlama API into your authentication flow.
Configure webhook endpoint to receive alerts.
entry.whenToUse: Use LoginLlama when you need basic suspicious login detection and webhook alerts for your app's authentication system.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced fraud analytics, custom rules, or guaranteed SLAs for enterprise-critical workloads.
entry.body: LoginLlama offers a free tier for suspicious login detection with webhook notifications, suitable for small projects and basic security needs.

entry.title: PropelAuth
entry.description: PropelAuth provides user authentication, authorization, and multi-tenant management for web and mobile applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes authentication for up to 1,000 monthly active users.
limits: Email/password, social, and magic link login
caveats: Custom domains and advanced SSO require paid plans
Support limited to email

useCases: Add authentication to web or mobile apps
quickstartSteps: Sign up at propelauth.com and create a new project.
Configure authentication methods (email/password, social, magic link) in the dashboard.
Integrate PropelAuth SDK into your app using provided guides.
Set up tenant management if building a multi-tenant app.
Test user signup and login flows locally.
entry.whenToUse: Use PropelAuth when you need quick, secure authentication and multi-tenant support without building auth from scratch.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced SSO, custom domains, or deep customization on the free tier.
entry.body: PropelAuth offers a generous free tier for authentication and multi-tenant management, making it ideal for startups and indie projects needing secure user auth without complex setup.

entry.title: SimpleLogin
entry.description: SimpleLogin provides email aliasing to protect your real email address and manage spam.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free plan includes up to 10 email aliases with unlimited bandwidth.
caveats: Custom domains require paid plan
Limited to one mailbox

useCases: Protecting your real email from spam
Signing up for online services safely
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free SimpleLogin account.
Verify your email address.
Add your primary mailbox for forwarding.
Create your first email alias.
Start using aliases to sign up for services.
entry.whenToUse: Use SimpleLogin when you want to shield your real email address and manage spam using aliases.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need custom domains or more than one mailbox on the free plan.
entry.body: SimpleLogin is an email aliasing service that helps you protect your real email address, reduce spam, and manage online privacy with ease.

entry.title: Stack Auth
entry.description: Stack Auth provides passwordless authentication and user management APIs for web and mobile applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes up to 2,000 monthly active users with core authentication features.
caveats: No custom branding on free tier
Limited support

useCases: Add passwordless login to web apps
Enable social login for mobile apps
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Stack Auth account.
Create a new application in the dashboard.
Configure allowed domains and callback URLs.
Integrate the SDK or API into your app.
Test authentication flow in your development environment.
entry.whenToUse: Use Stack Auth when you need simple, passwordless authentication and user management for web or mobile projects.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced enterprise features, custom branding, or on-premise deployment.
entry.body: Stack Auth offers a developer-friendly platform for adding passwordless authentication and user management to applications, with a generous free tier for small projects.

entry.title: Supabase Auth
entry.description: Supabase Auth provides authentication and user management with social logins, email, and magic links for web and mobile apps.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 50,000 monthly active users and unlimited social logins.
caveats: Email sending uses shared IP pools
No custom SMTP on free tier

useCases: Add authentication to web or mobile apps
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Supabase account.
Create a new project in the Supabase dashboard.
Navigate to the Auth section and configure providers (email, Google, etc.).
Integrate Supabase Auth SDK into your app.
Test user sign-up and login flows.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need simple, scalable authentication with social logins and email/password for web or mobile apps.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need advanced enterprise features, custom SMTP, or on-premise deployment.
entry.body: Supabase Auth offers a developer-friendly authentication solution with generous free limits, supporting social logins, email, and passwordless flows for modern applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes up to 7,000 active users and unlimited logins per month.
caveats: No SLA on free tier

Advanced features require paid plan

useCases: Add login/signup to web or mobile apps

entry.title: Authgear
entry.description: Authgear provides user authentication, social login, and identity management as a service for web and mobile applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes up to 1,000 monthly active users with core authentication features.
caveats: No custom domain on free tier
Limited support

useCases: Add user authentication to web or mobile apps
quickstartSteps: Sign up for an Authgear account.
Create a new application in the Authgear dashboard.
Configure redirect URIs and authentication settings.
Integrate Authgear SDK into your app.
Test user signup and login flows.
entry.whenToUse: Use Authgear when you need to quickly add secure authentication and social login to your web or mobile app without managing infrastructure.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced customization, on-premise deployment, or need support for more than 1,000 monthly active users on the free tier.
entry.body: Authgear offers a developer-friendly authentication platform with a generous free tier, making it easy to add secure login and identity management to your apps.

entry.title: Authress
entry.description: Authress provides user authentication, authorization, and access control as a managed service for web and API applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes up to 100 monthly active users with core authentication and authorization features.
caveats: No SAML or enterprise SSO in free tier
Support limited to documentation and community

useCases: Add login and registration to web apps
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Authress account.
Register your application in the Authress dashboard.
Configure OAuth providers and callback URLs.
Integrate Authress SDK or API into your app.
Test user login and access control flows.
entry.whenToUse: Use Authress when you need managed authentication and fine-grained access control for web or API projects without building your own auth system.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require SAML/enterprise SSO, advanced custom workflows, or need to support more than 100 users for free.
avoidIf: expect rapid user growth beyond free tier
entry.body: Authress is a managed authentication and authorization service that enables developers to quickly add secure login, user management, and access control to applications. The always-free tier supports up to 100 monthly active users, making it suitable for small projects and early-stage startups. It offers OAuth integration, basic access control, and unlimited applications, but does not include enterprise SSO or advanced identity federation in the free plan. Authress is easy to set up and integrates with popular frameworks, making it a practical choice for teams seeking to avoid the complexity of building their own authentication system.

entry.title: Authy
entry.description: Authy provides two-factor authentication (2FA) services for securing user logins and sensitive operations.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes up to 100 authentications per month.
caveats: Phone/SMS/voice delivery may incur charges after free tier
Requires Twilio account
billingRiskNotes: Charges apply if you exceed free authentication limit

useCases: Add 2FA to web or mobile apps
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Twilio account and enable Authy.
Create an Authy application in the Twilio Console.
Integrate the Authy API into your app using provided credentials.
Configure user registration and verification flows for 2FA.
Test authentication using SMS, voice, or push methods.
entry.whenToUse: Use Authy when you need to quickly add 2FA to your app with minimal setup and support for multiple delivery methods.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Authy if you require unlimited free authentications or need a self-hosted 2FA solution.
entry.body: Authy by Twilio offers a simple way to add two-factor authentication to applications, supporting SMS, voice, and push notifications with a generous free tier for small projects.

entry.title: Azure Active Directory B2C
entry.description: Azure Active Directory B2C provides cloud-based identity and access management for customer-facing applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: 50,000 monthly active users (MAU) free for standard authentication.
caveats: Premium features and multi-factor authentication incur charges
External identity providers may have additional costs
billingRiskNotes: Charges apply if MAU exceeds 50,000 or premium features are enabled

useCases: Add user sign-up and sign-in to web or mobile apps
Support social and enterprise identity providers
quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure portal and create an Azure AD B2C tenant.
Link your B2C tenant to your Azure subscription.
Register your application in the B2C tenant.
Configure user flows (sign-up, sign-in, password reset).
Integrate the B2C authentication endpoints in your app.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need scalable, managed authentication for customer-facing apps with support for social and enterprise identities.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require fully on-premises identity management or need advanced features without incurring extra costs.
entry.body: Azure Active Directory B2C enables secure, scalable authentication for customer-facing applications with support for social and enterprise identity providers. The free tier covers up to 50,000 monthly active users for standard authentication, making it suitable for most startups and mid-sized projects. Premium features and higher usage incur additional costs, so monitor usage to avoid unexpected charges. Integration requires Azure portal setup and application registration, but provides robust user management and compliance features.

entry.title: Firebase Auth
entry.description: Firebase Auth provides authentication services for web and mobile apps with support for multiple identity providers.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes generous monthly quotas for authentication operations.
caveats: Phone auth limits vary by country
Overages billed per verification
billingRiskNotes: Phone authentication overages can incur unexpected costs

quickstartSteps: Create a Firebase project at the Firebase Console.
Add your app (web, iOS, or Android) to the project.
Enable desired authentication methods in the Authentication section.
Integrate Firebase Auth SDK into your app.
Implement sign-in and sign-up flows using the SDK.
entry.whenToUse: Use Firebase Auth when you need quick, secure authentication for web or mobile apps with minimal backend setup.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require custom authentication flows, on-premise hosting, or need to avoid vendor lock-in.
entry.body: Firebase Auth offers a simple way to add authentication to your app, supporting email/password, social logins, and anonymous access with a generous free tier.

entry.title: LoginLlama
entry.description: LoginLlama provides fraud detection for user logins via a simple API integration.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 1,000 login checks per month.
caveats: Requires API integration
Limited to login events only

useCases: Adding risk scoring to authentication flows
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a LoginLlama account.
Obtain your API key from the dashboard.
Integrate the LoginLlama API into your login flow.
Send login event data to the API and receive risk scores.
Act on risk scores to block or flag suspicious logins.
entry.whenToUse: Use LoginLlama when you need to add fraud detection to your application's login process with minimal setup.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require fraud detection for non-login events or need on-premise deployment.
entry.body: LoginLlama offers a simple API to detect and score potentially fraudulent login attempts, helping developers protect user accounts with minimal integration effort.

entry.title: PropelAuth
entry.description: PropelAuth provides user authentication, multi-tenancy, and user management APIs for web and SaaS applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes authentication, user management, and multi-tenancy for up to 1,000 monthly active users.
caveats: No SAML/SSO or advanced integrations on free tier
Support is community-based

quickstartSteps: Sign up at propelauth.com and create a new project.
Configure authentication methods (email/password, social login) in the dashboard.
Copy your project credentials (API key, domain) from the dashboard.
Install the PropelAuth SDK in your app (e.g., npm install propelauth).
Integrate authentication flows using provided SDK methods.
entry.whenToUse: Use PropelAuth when you need fast, secure user authentication and multi-tenancy for SaaS or web apps without building auth from scratch.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced enterprise SSO (SAML, OIDC) or need on-premises/self-hosted authentication solutions.
entry.body: PropelAuth offers a developer-friendly authentication platform with built-in multi-tenancy, making it easy to add secure login and user management to SaaS applications.

entry.title: SimpleLogin
entry.description: SimpleLogin provides email aliasing to protect your real email address and manage spam.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier allows up to 10 active email aliases with unlimited replies and bandwidth.
caveats: Custom domain support requires paid plan
No catch-all aliases on free tier

useCases: Protecting your real email from spam
Signing up for online services safely
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free SimpleLogin account at simplelogin.io.
Verify your real email address.
Create your first email alias from the dashboard.
Use the alias to sign up for a service or website.
Manage or disable aliases as needed from your dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use SimpleLogin when you want to shield your real email address and control spam from online signups.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need custom domain aliases or more than 10 aliases without upgrading to a paid plan.
entry.body: SimpleLogin is an email aliasing service that helps users protect their real email address, reduce spam, and manage online identities with ease.

entry.title: Stack Auth
entry.description: Stack Auth provides passwordless authentication APIs and SDKs for web and mobile applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes authentication for up to 2,000 monthly active users.
caveats: Custom domains and advanced integrations require paid plans

useCases: Add passwordless login to web apps
Enable social login for mobile apps
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Stack Auth account.
Create a new application in the dashboard.
Configure allowed domains and callback URLs.
Integrate the SDK or API into your app.
Test authentication flow in your development environment.
entry.whenToUse: Use Stack Auth when you need quick, secure passwordless authentication for web or mobile apps without managing user credentials.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require on-premise deployment, advanced enterprise features, or full control over authentication infrastructure.
entry.body: Stack Auth offers a simple, secure passwordless authentication platform with a generous free tier for small projects and early-stage apps.

entry.title: Supabase Auth
entry.description: Supabase Auth provides user authentication and authorization with email, social logins, and magic links for web and mobile apps.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes up to 50,000 monthly active users with unlimited social logins.
caveats: Email sending is rate-limited and uses Supabase's shared email provider.
Enterprise SSO and custom SMTP require paid plans.

useCases: Add secure authentication to web or mobile apps
Implement passwordless login with magic links
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Supabase account.
Create a new project in the Supabase dashboard.
Navigate to the Auth section and configure providers (email, social, etc.).
Integrate Supabase Auth client library into your app.
Test user signup and login flows.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need fast, managed authentication with email, social, or magic link support for web or mobile projects.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require enterprise SSO, custom SMTP, or advanced auth flows not supported by Supabase's free tier.
entry.body: Supabase Auth offers a scalable, developer-friendly authentication solution with generous free tier limits, supporting email, social, and passwordless login methods.
